meaning: adj.固定的,不动的,不改变的
examples: On one issue, however, she was immovable.
On this issue he is completely immovable.
The Pope now seems immovable on the issue.
When Johannes Gutenberg invented movable type a half-millennium ago, he also gave us immovable text.
This peculiar tension, immovable object and irresistible force, is unlikely to change before election day.
